items.title: Several infrastructure projects put on backburner as ACT 'at peak construction'

children.content: Some ACT government infrastructure projects, including health, emergency services and sports facilities in North Gungahlin, will be deferred to save about $700 million over four years.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a navy suit jacket and glasses sits indoors speaking with his hands.

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a navy suit jacket and glasses sits indoors speaking with his hands.

items.title: Even kids want gambling ads banned, so why won't the government do it?

children.content: The government's proposed crackdown on gambling ads doesn't go as far as most Australians want. When you run the numbers, it's a win for the betting industry.

image.altText: Hands holding a mobile phone displaying a gambling app

items.title: Knitters find key to serenity during 'party, party, party' snow season

children.content: As the NSW ski season gets underway, Jindabyne's Snowy Stitchers Social Club finds connection and mindfulness in needles and yarn.

image.altText: Rachelle Edwards, wearing a knitted beanie and gloves, standings in front of a snow field.

items.title: 'Cybertongue' could prevent 70 million tonnes of milk waste every year

children.content: About a sixth of the world's milk is wasted, over 150 million tonnes every year, but a Canberra company has come up with new technology that could drastically reduce that.

gallery.altText: A line of two-litre milk bottles in a processing facility.

thumbnail.altText: A line of two-litre milk bottles in a processing facility.

items.title: Canberra man facing almost 70 sexual assault, violence charges

children.content: Police said multiple women contacted them in late 2025 and early 2026 to accuse the man of sexual assault while he was in relationships with them.

image.altText: A man with his hands cuffed behind his back is led away by a police officer.

items.title: Brumbies set unwanted records as winless Aussie finals run continues

children.content: The ACT Brumbies have been dumped out of the Super Rugby Pacific finals series after setting unwanted club records in Wellington on Friday.

image.altText: Rugby players with hands on hips, looking dejected

items.title: Canberra Liberals say alleged violent threat was a 'misunderstanding'

children.content: The Canberra Liberals have distanced themselves from former leader Leanne Castley's allegation that she was threatened with physical assault, saying the remark related to an "animated discussion" that was quickly resolved.

gallery.altText: Two politicians speak at a press conference.

thumbnail.altText: Two politicians speak at a press conference.

items.title: Trump storms out of interview after grilling on false election claims

children.content: US President Donald Trump angrily storms out of an interview with American broadcaster NBC after he was questioned about his unfounded claims of election fraud.

image.altText: A man and a woman sit in a barn, facing each other for an interview.

items.title: It's an AI behemoth. Now Anthropic is making a power play in Australia

children.content: The world's biggest tech titans see Australia as a promising investment opportunity for their AI ambitions, but some fear the federal government's regulatory retreat could cause more harm.

articles.title: Anthropic wants caution but the market wants more AI

children.content: Anthropic wants caution as AI intelligence surges but the stock market can't get enough.

image.altText: Anthropic logo on a smartphone screen

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

gallery.altText: A person holding an iPhone with Character.aI, ChatGPT, Claude and Grok chatbot apps on screen.

thumbnail.altText: A person holding an iPhone with Character.aI, ChatGPT, Claude and Grok chatbot apps on screen.

items.title: Top neurologists say regulator ignored warnings as weed killer decision looms

children.content: Australia's leading movement disorder specialists say the chemical regulator has not engaged with their concerns about paraquat, despite growing evidence linking the herbicide to Parkinson's disease.

image.altText: A man wearing dark jumper and beige puffer coat speaks, seen from behind the shoulder of someone wearing purple

items.title: Slater to play peacemaker after Maroons stars' fiery club clash

children.content: Queensland coach Billy Slater jokes he might need to table a peace summit between Maroons captain Cameron Munster and fullback Kalyn Ponga as he reveals his team for State of Origin II. Follow live.

image.altText: Cameron Munster shoves Kalyn Ponga during an NRL game between the Melbourne Storm and the Newcastle Knights.

items.title: The electric classic car that now drives like a 'pocket rocket'

children.content: It may look like a small vintage car, but an EV conversion has brought it "up to speed" to its modern counterparts.

gallery.altText: David Dyer kneeling next to blue small classic car
Blue small car on the road

thumbnail.altText: David Dyer kneeling next to blue small classic car
Blue small car on the road

avatar.alt: Millie Roberts wearing a pink top and earrings and an 'M' necklace

items.title: From migration to AUKUS, more politicians are speaking out

children.content: Former Coalition frontbencher Paul Scarr says inflammatory migration rhetoric is taking a toll on migrant communities. His warning comes as both major parties wrestle with populist pressures.

image.altText: Man standing in a suit in the senate

items.title: Indigenous families want 'crisis' investigated after 15 deaths in six months

children.content: Remote Indigenous communities across Australia are painfully aware that their life expectancy rates are worsening — and in one Northern Territory community, residents are calling for an investigation into a local death rate they feel is "not normal".

image.altText: Aboriginal senior woman and younger Aboriginal man sitting together, serious expression, in front of green leaves.

items.title: Perth man wants compensation for forensic examination damage

children.content: Troy Coward's investment property was the scene of a brutal murder involving his tenants in 2012. More than 13 years later, he is fighting for compensation from police over the impact he says their investigation had on his health and finances.

image.altText: Man with short hair and dark brown blazer, arms folded, with trees and pathway in background

items.title: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan decries sexist Melbourne billboard

children.content: Premier Jacinta Allan has been joined by Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and former prime minister Julia Gillard, among other politicians, in denouncing the language used to describe her on political signs seen in the Melbourne CBD.

image.altText: Jacinta Allan has a neutral expression at a press conference outside parliament on February 19, 2026.

items.title: With his pet chihuahua and trusty gopher, 97yo grazier won't slow down

children.content: At 97, Doug Harrison still scoots around his outback station on a gopher, doing as much work as he can. As he nears a century of living, he reflects on memorable moments over the decades, with a cuppa in hand and his trusty chihuahua by his side.

gallery.altText: a man with black sunglasses holding the steering wheel of a vehicle with a white small dog sitting next to him
an old man with black sunglasses and a red face close to the camera looking

thumbnail.altText: a man with black sunglasses holding the steering wheel of a vehicle with a white small dog sitting next to him
an old man with black sunglasses and a red face close to the camera looking

items.title: History quiz: When was the first Mars Exploration Rover launch?

children.content: What make of car was stolen in the world's first official vehicle theft? And do you know where the Endeavour was repaired after hitting the Great Barrier Reef in 1770?

image.altText: A black and white image of a robot arm surveying the rocky, barren surface of Mars.

items.title: Shrek, Captain Cook and the stars: Welcome to Brian Robinson's multiverse

children.content: Newcastle Art Gallery presents the distinctive and intricate work of the Torres Strait Islander artist in its first major solo survey following a four-year refurbishment.

image.altText: A Torres Strait Islander man with a long grey beard and an earring in his right ear, wearing a black jacket.

items.title: Maths genius, devoted mum, headline-making CEO on King's Birthday Honours roll

children.content: Almost 950 Australians are honoured, including five stand-out recipients who are now Companions of the Order.

gallery.altText: A graphic with the images of three women and two men.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic with the images of three women and two men.

items.title: Does being in a 'safe seat' influence whether your hospital gets an upgrade?

children.content: Are hospitals in safe seats being overlooked for multi-million-dollar refurbishments by the Victorian government?

image.altText: Warrnambool base hospital

items.title: Obscene acts charges prompt calls for more CCTV

children.content: A woman who reported a terrifying incident while walking at night in Derby says more CCTV cameras would increase safety and help monitor popular locations.

image.altText: The main strip of Derby, a narrow road surrounded by red dirt and bushy trees.

items.title: Quick hits: Monaco GP interrupted for safety as Kardashians keep up with F1

children.content: From a dodgy section of track to a mega celebrity watching her partner equal a record with the great Ayrton Senna, the 2026 Monaco Grand Prix had plenty of talking points.

image.altText: Kim Kardashian looks on in parc ferme at Monaco

items.title: Piastri promoted after penalty as teen sensation wins wild Monaco GP

children.content: Teenage sensation Kimi Antonelli was flawless to lead every lap of a drama-filled Monaco Grand Prix, claiming a fifth consecutive win to extend his lead in the Formula 1 drivers' championship. Follow live.

image.altText: Oscar Piastri at the Monaco Grand Prix 2026

items.title: Why sentiment in Japan has turned against foreigners despite a worker shortage

children.content: Japan's answer to One Nation skyrocketed in popularity after adopting an anti-immigration stance despite Japan needing foreign workers more than ever.

gallery.altText: Sohei Kamiya, holding two microphones tape together, speaking with a closed fist in the air.

thumbnail.altText: Sohei Kamiya, holding two microphones tape together, speaking with a closed fist in the air.

items.title: Disability community says NDIS 'fighting the wrong battles' over funding plans

children.content: The agency spent $60 million on lawyers to fight participants at a tribunal last financial year. Disability advocates say there should be greater focus on the NDIS's legal fees as cuts to the scheme are being made.

image.altText: A man and a woman both in wheelchair going down a walkway with trees

items.title: Victorian teachers sue over alleged unpaid superannuation

children.content: A class action alleges the Education Department short-changed teachers some $100 million in superannuation contributions between 1995 and 2023.

image.altText: A woman with chin length wavy blonde hair with a two-toned orange scarf around her neck sits with patchworking on her lap.

items.title: Players left in tears as Danish star Eriksen collapses during friendly

children.content: Danish player Christian Eriksen collapses on the pitch in a match against Ukraine but is conscious, the Danish Football Association says in a social media post.

image.altText: Danish players show their distress after Christian Eriksen's collapse.

items.title: 'Cherished' Bulls player and ex-Michael Jordan teammate dies

children.content: Stacey King, who won NBA titles with the Chicago Bulls in the 1990s and was a longtime broadcaster for the team, has died.

image.altText: Stacey King playing for the Chicago Bulls in 1990.

items.title: How this Indigenous mother paved the way for her children to study medicine

children.content: Ieesha, Abdul and Jamaal Nasser are following in the footsteps of their mother Tatum Maybir, who entered medicine aged 45 after raising her six children.

items.title: Outback cake decorator stops reporting vandalism because 'nothing gets done'

children.content: After three vandalism incidents in three years, cake decorator Ishani Harney says the emotional and financial toll of crime lingers long after the damage is repaired.

image.altText: Mount Isa cake decorator Ishani Harney holds a shard of glass from her shopfront window that was broken during a 2023 break-in.

items.title: Feral pigs raid NSW farms, tearing up hectares of agricultural land

children.content: After an extended dry spell, farmers across inland NSW are reporting significant damage to their paddocks by feral pigs seeking food.

image.altText: A light-brown half-grown feral pig stands in mud.

items.title: Qld government contemplates ditching anti-junk-mail measures

children.content: A new consultation paper argues the measures are too burdensome and recommends repealing state laws and making local councils responsible for combating junk mail.

image.altText: A grid of apartment letterboxes with 'no junk mail' stickers on each.

items.title: Cyclist injured after collision with police vehicle on Tasmania's east coast

children.content: Police say a woman riding a motorised bicycle sustained serious injuries in the crash at St Helens.

image.altText: Tasmania Police vehicles.

items.title: Highland cows encounters bring 'positive impact', agritourism operators say

children.content: Traipsing around a farm in the wake of a highland cow or donkey might seem like a quaint rural pastime — but those in the business of agritourism believe there are other benefits.

gallery.altText: Two large shaggy cows in a green paddock. One is looking directly atthe camera, the other is grazing the grass.
A young man, his female partner and their small baby with a brown fluffy highland calf and a white calf.

thumbnail.altText: Two large shaggy cows in a green paddock. One is looking directly atthe camera, the other is grazing the grass.
A young man, his female partner and their small baby with a brown fluffy highland calf and a white calf.
